text.title: practices-backend-languages Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for practices-backend-languages as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Interface defined in the producer package: `package userservice; type UserRepository interface {...}`.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Violates Go's "accept interfaces, return structs" idiom. Causes import cycles and forces the producer to anticipate all consumers.

failure-mode.description: `@Autowired` on a field: `@Autowired private UserRepository userRepository;`.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Field injection requires Spring context in tests; constructor injection lets tests pass mocks directly.

failure-mode.description: Using `reqwest::blocking::Client` inside `#[tokio::main]` or any async function.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Compiles and runs, but blocks the async runtime thread, causing deadlocks under concurrent load. Use `reqwest::Client` (async) instead.
